text: Welcome to the minute of speech. This
confusing word pair is a request from
Vietnam. The words are treasure,
valuables kept in hiding, and pressure,
the feeling of stressful urgency. To say
these words correctly, focus on both the
first sounds T and P and the middle
sounds
and sh. To say treasure, start by saying
tr. Do this by touching the tip of the
tongue to the back of the top front
teeth for the t. Then pull it down for
the r. Your lips will be slightly
rounded. Move to the short vowel e. Add
a made with your tongue in the middle of
your mouth while your lips are rounded
and your voice is on. Your voice must be
on or it will sound like shh instead of
end with the
treasure.
To say pressure, start by saying pr. Do
this by pressing your lips together for
the p then moving to the r by opening
and slightly rounding the lips. Move to
the short vowel e. Add a shh which is
made with your tongue in the middle of
your mouth while your lips are rounded
and your voice is off. Shh. End with the
pressure. Let's try it. Treasure.
Treasure.
Treasure.
Pressure.
Pressure.
Pressure.
Treasure.
Pressure.
He was under extreme pressure to locate
the treasure. He was under extreme
pressure to locate the treasure. Try it.
People will notice the difference.
